description: A published catalog of one organization’s detection hardware. Any operator — vendor, research group, or institution — publishes its own manifest against this schema so datasets from different sensor arrays can be compared directly.

sensorType.description: The `@disclosureos/records` SensorType this sensor maps to.

detectionMethod.description: The `@disclosureos/records` DetectionMethod this sensor maps to.

proposedSensorType.description: True = sensorType is not in the records enum yet and is proposed by the publisher.

proposedDetectionMethod.description: True = detectionMethod is not in the records enum yet and is proposed by the publisher.

timeSource.description: Clock source that timestamps the sensor’s data.

timeUncertaintyNs.description: Timestamp uncertainty in nanoseconds. Null = not yet characterized.

locatorPattern.description: Storage key pattern or path convention for locating the raw files.

sampleRateHz.description: Sample rate in Hz, where applicable.

anyOf.description: GUM uncertainty evaluation type: A (statistical) or B (other means).

uncertainty.description: GUM-style measurement uncertainty. Fields are nullable until an uncertainty budget exists — declaring "unknown" honestly is itself information.

status.description: Calibration maturity gradient: none = no calibration; candidate_identified = a reference is named but not used; in_practice = a real method is performed but not fully budgeted; documented = method + uncertainty budget published.

calibrated.description: Coarse calibrated flag, mirroring `SensorReading.calibrated`. Null = undetermined.

currentMethod.description: What the operator actually does today. Null = no calibration currently performed.

traceableReference.description: Traceable reference, in use or identified as a candidate (e.g. "GPS/UTC", "USGS/INTERMAGNET").

referenceInUse.description: True = the traceable reference is part of current practice. False = identified candidate only. Null = undetermined.

id.description: Stable kebab-case id, unique within the manifest; addressable from a record via `SensorReading.sensorRef` ("<org-slug>:<sensor-id>").

modality.description: Top-level sensing modality the sensor belongs to.

manufacturerModel.description: Hardware class descriptor. Publishers MAY withhold specific make/model identities and publish spec-level class descriptors instead (e.g. "dual GM-tube counter" rather than a product name).

inHouse.description: True if operator-built rather than off-the-shelf.

compute.description: The compute unit this sensor is attached to, for platforms with several compute nodes.

recordsMapping.description: How this sensor maps to the `SensorReading` vocabulary in `@disclosureos/records`. Values outside the records enums must carry the matching proposed* flag.

timing.description: Timing provenance. Timestamp error of even a few seconds confounds multi-station correlation, so the clock source must be declared, not assumed.

rawData.description: Raw-data format and locator. Supports a metadata-first architecture: circulate manifests widely, pull raw data on demand.

items.description: One measured quantity with its unit and uncertainty.

calibration.description: Calibration provenance. Deliberately separates what an operator actually does today from the reference it has identified; honest nulls are first-class, so calibration gaps stay visible and comparable across operators.

specs.description: Free-form hardware specifications from the operator’s spec sheet (resolution, range, sensitivity, interface, …). Keys vary by modality.

modality.description: Top-level sensing modality the sensor belongs to.

status.description: Acquisition status of a declared future hardware upgrade.

link.description: Public product or reference page, where the publisher chooses to share one.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the sensor-manifest schema this instance targets (SemVer).

orgSlug.description: Stable lowercase slug for the publisher (e.g. "eldaeon"); the org half of a `sensorRef`.

generatedAt.description: ISO date the manifest was generated.

items.description: One sensor: its class, spec, timing, raw-data locator, measurements, and calibration provenance.
Planned hardware declared ahead of deployment so data consumers can anticipate new streams. Not part of the live sensor array.
